question: Looking at the US population map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: Iowa and Massachusetts appear to have similar populations because they share the same color category, but their actual populations might still differ substantially within that range.
options.B: North Dakota and Maryland clearly have nearly identical populations since they fall into different color categories.
options.C: Iowa and Massachusetts have similar populations.
options.D: The population differences between these states are impossible to determine from this visualization.

explanation: Iowa (12.8M) and Massachusetts (19.2M) have a 6.4M population difference, while North Dakota (5.8M) and Maryland (6.3M) have only a 0.5M difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Iowa and Massachusetts into the same color bin (12-20M range), making their large difference appear similar, while North Dakota and Maryland appear more different due to different color bins.
